t_ch: 这种小事你们都办不到吗？
t_en: You all can't even handle such trifles?

t_ch: 非常抱歉，夫人。
t_en: I'm truly sorry, madam.

t_ch: 我们已经为您重新预约了。
t_en: We've rebooked your reservation.

t_ch: 我可以付出一切代价……
t_en: I'd be willing to pay any price...

t_ch: 那里可以满足你的一切心愿。
t_en: That place can grant your every wish.

t_ch: 非常抱歉，给您添麻烦了……
t_en: Really sorry to cause you trouble...

t_ch: 我们保证不会再有这种事发生。
t_en: We guarantee that such an incident will never happen again.

t_ch: 害我们行程推迟！
t_en: You've caused us to have to push back our itinerary!

t_ch: 道歉有用的话……
t_en: If apologies could fix things...

t_ch: 这是知更鸟小姐的嘱咐。
t_en: These were Miss Robin's instructions.

t_ch: 这个…我回去准备一下。
t_en: I... I'll return to prepare.

t_ch: 嗯，这件事我也有所耳闻。
t_en: Yeah, I also heard about this.

t_ch: 这明明是我提的方案…
t_en: I was the one who proposed this...

t_ch: 放心，交给我也一样。
t_en: Don't worry. It's all the same if I handle it.

t_ch: 二位，请往这边请。
t_en: Please, this way.

t_ch: 这件事影响巨大……
t_en: This matter has grave repercussions...

t_ch: 我觉得家族未必会接受。
t_en: I doubt The Family will agree.

t_ch: 我问你，你有家人吗？
t_en: I'm asking you if you have family.

t_ch: 呃…我有？
t_en: Er... I do?

t_ch: 很好，你合格了！
t_en: Good, you pass!

t_ch: …我是来报名加入赛车社的，这和家人有什么关系？
t_en: ...I'm here to sign up for the Racing Club. What does it have to do with family?

t_ch: 听好了——没有什么比家人更强大。
t_en: Listen up — there's nothing stronger than family.

t_ch: 一切为了，家人！！！
t_en: All for my family!!!

t_ch: 你们两个，要不要转来财富学院？
t_en: You two, do you want to transfer to Fortune Academy?

t_ch: 这里的老师都很负责，课程质量也相当高，学习氛围浓厚。想变得更加优秀，来财富学院就对了！
t_en: The teachers here are very responsible. The course quality is excellent and the learning atmosphere is strong. If you want to better yourself, Fortune Academy is for you!

t_ch: 财富学院有早八的课吗？
t_en: Does Fortune Academy have eight AM classes?

t_ch: 财富学院会帮毕业生安排工作吗？
t_en: Does Fortune Academy help students with job placements after graduation?

t_ch: 这个……
t_en: Well...

t_ch: 早跟你们说过吧，苏打豆汁儿可不是一般人能消受的……
t_en: I told you, not everyone can accept mung bean soda...

t_ch: 那不是仙舟名产吗…呕…总得尝尝……
t_en: Isn't this a famous delicacy from the Xianzhou... Ugh... Gotta try it at least...

t_ch: 拉倒吧，别说咱们这些外地人受不了，那玩意在仙舟也不怎么受待见。
t_en: Forget it. Not only do we outsiders find it hard to stomach, it's not even well-received on the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 我也就当初年少无知，不小心喝过一口，那味道…实在惊悚，给我留下了不可磨灭的心理阴影。
t_en: I was young and naïve back then and accidentally took a sip. The taste was... truly horrifying and left me with actual trauma.

t_ch: 听你这么一说，我倒想试试了……
t_en: After hearing you say that, I kinda wanna try it....

t_ch: 完了，论文查重没通过，我要延期毕业了。
t_en: I'm doomed. My thesis didn't pass the plagiarism check. I'll have to postpone my graduation.

t_ch: 啊？你的查重率是多少？我也借鉴了别人的论文，不会被发现吧？
t_en: Ah? What was your plagiarism rate? I also referenced other papers. Do you think I'll be exposed?

t_ch: 百分之八十…唉，谁能想到呢，学院居然引进了星际和平公司最新的论文查重系统，这下没法蒙混过关了。
t_en: Eighty percent... *sigh* Who knew that the academy would introduce the latest plagiarism check system from the IPC. I can't smoke my way through this time.

t_ch: 你这是直接复制过来了吧，倒是稍微改改啊！
t_en: You're just directly copying it! At least tweak it a little!

t_ch: 喝下这瓶苏乐达~快乐美梦速速达~
t_en: Drink this SoulGlad. Make your dreams a blast!

t_ch: …她是怎么考进谐乐学院的啊？
t_en: ...How did she even make it into Charmony Academy?

t_ch: 嘘！别问不该问的事，现在只管鼓掌就行了！
t_en: Shh! Don't ask things you shouldn't. Just clap!

t_ch: 你好~可以请你讲讲对折纸大学的看法吗？
t_en: Hello! Can you tell us more about what you think about Paperfold University?

t_ch: 哈？这所学校有什么好讲的，课程质量良莠不齐，毕业又不好找工作……
t_en: Huh? What is there to say about this academy? The course quality is inconsistent and it's hard to find a job after graduation...

t_ch: 等等，你这是不是要上电视啊？
t_en: Wait, is this going on TV?

t_ch: 呃…是的。
t_en: Er... Yes.

t_ch: 这段麻烦重新录——大家好，我是折纸大学的毕业生，我深沉地爱着这所学校。
t_en: Let's re-record this part — hello everyone, I'm a graduate of Paperfold University and I have a deep love for this school.

t_ch: 啊啊啊我的宝物！我的限量典藏版《银河球棒侠》卡带——
t_en: Ahhh my treasure! My limited-edition collectible, the Galactic Baseballer cartridge...

t_ch: 别嚎了，仔细想想你放哪了？
t_en: Stop wailing and carefully recall where you put it!

t_ch: 我…明明下课前把它放在抽屉里，怎么就没了呢？
t_en: I... I clearly put it in the drawer before class ended. Where would it have gone?

t_ch: …整天丢三落四的，实在不行就去广播站发个寻物启事，让别人帮忙找找吧。
t_en: ...You're always losing stuff. If you really can't find it, just go to the broadcast station and put out a lost-and-found notice, so someone can help you look for it.

t_ch: 不行！告诉学校的话，我在课堂上玩游戏的事不就暴露了吗！
t_en: No! If I tell the school, won't it expose that I've been gaming in class?

t_ch: …我是帮朋友代领，不是偷快递，你们要相信我啊！
t_en: ...I was just picking it up for a friend and not stealing the package! You have to believe me!

t_ch: 那就说说看，这个包裹的收件人是谁？
t_en: Then let's hear it. Who is the recipient of this package?

t_ch: 我记得是…波、波奇？
t_en: I remember... Bo-Bocchi?

t_ch: 哼哼，连对方的名字都说不出来，就别再虚构朋友啦。
t_en: Hmph, you can't even tell me the recipient's name. Stop making up fake friends.

t_ch: 老实交代！最近我们的外卖离奇消失，是不是也被你偷了！
t_en: Be honest! Our takeouts have been mysteriously disappearing recently. Did you steal them?

t_ch: 我以为在学校里避嫌就好，没想到在艾迪恩公园还能碰见自己的学生。
t_en: I thought I'd be fine if I just stayed away from gossip at school — I wasn't expecting to run into my own students at Aideen Park.

t_ch: 哈哈，我看见他们偷拍的照片了，别说，拍得还挺好。
t_en: Haha, I saw the photos they secretly took. Honestly, they were quite well taken.

t_ch: …你能靠谱点吗？等事情闹到学院那边去，看你还笑不笑得出来。
t_en: ...Can you be serious? If this goes back to the academy, let's see if you can still laugh about it.

t_ch: 要我说，咱俩不如直接官宣得了，反正没有规定教师间不能恋爱，大不了我主动避嫌，转去谐乐学院呗。
t_en: If you ask me, we might as well make it official. There's no rule against teachers dating. If needed, I'll just transfer to Charmony Academy to avoid gossip.

t_ch: 可你不是一直想成为财富学院代表吗…哎，果然办公室恋情不可取啊。
t_en: But you've always wanted to be the representative of Fortune Academy... *sigh* It seems like office romances are impossible.

t_ch: 这两个人是怎么回事？我入学时候就见他俩在吵架，现在快毕业了，居然还没吵完？
t_en: What's wrong with those two? They've been quarreling since the day I enrolled. It's almost time for graduation, and they are still at it?

t_ch: 都是延毕的学生，在争社团部长的位置。哎，他们那是没新人了吗？
t_en: They both delayed their graduation and are arguing over the club president role. Are there no new candidates?

t_ch: 嘿，折纸大学的格拉克斯校长正瞧着你呢，仿佛在说——匹诺康尼的未来就在你的手中！
t_en: Hey, Paperfold University's Dean Glaux is looking at you, as if saying: Penacony's future is in your hands!

t_ch: 海报下方用歪歪扭扭的文字写着——「禁止在画像上涂鸦，违反者将被罚去擦洗校长雕像！格拉克斯校长是最伟大的啾！」
t_en: The squiggly words at the bottom of the poster say: "Graffiti prohibited on the portrait. Those found violating this rule will clean the dean's statue as punishment! Dean Glaux is the greatest! Tweet!"

t_ch: 瞧瞧这窜动的人群、看看这琳琅的摊位，简直完美符合你对大学社团的一切向往——好吧，虽然在来到这里之前，你从未对大学生活有过任何概念。
t_en: Just look at the thronging crowds and the dazzling stalls. It's a sight that perfectly matches your aspirations as to what college clubs should be, even if you had no idea what college life would be like before even setting foot here.

t_ch: 快瞧瞧那几面还空着的墙，你就不觉得手痒吗？
t_en: Quick, look at those blank walls. Don't you feel even a little antsy?

t_ch: 看上去应该是无害的涂鸦，但不论是什么图案，校方往往都会认为其中含有危害校园的元素。
t_en: They look like harmless graffiti, but no matter what their designs are, the school will always assume there are elements within that carry harmful messages.

t_ch: 看似普通的涂鸦，彰显着学生们无处安放的才华——尽管后勤部门从不这么认为。
t_en: What looks like ordinary graffiti is a testament to the aimless talent radiating from students — even if the Logistics Department doesn't think so.

t_ch: 这是克劳克影视乐园，筑梦学院优秀毕业生的作品——你看了半天才认出来，画得还挺别致。
t_en: This is Clock Studios Theme Park, the work of Dreamweaver Academy's exemplary graduates — you inspect it for quite a while before realizing what the topic is. The art is quite unconventional.

t_ch: 克劳克影城的设计图手稿，筑梦学院杰出学员的代表作之一。作为最早期的设计版本，和现在有些区别很正常。
t_en: Clock Studios Theme Park's design manual is one of the representative works of the Dreamweaver Academy's exemplary students. As an early design manuscript, it is only normal that the current version differs from it somewhat.

t_ch: 直到现在你依旧没弄清「白日梦」酒店大堂里那个漂浮的钟表究竟有什么用，总不能是单纯为了好看吧？
t_en: Even now, you still can't figure out what that floating clock is for in the Reverie's lobby. It can't just be for decor, can it?

t_ch: 酒店大堂钟表的设计图，解释了一切基于美学的设计思路细节，却从没提过这些离散的部件是靠什么浮在那里的。
t_en: The hotel lobby clock's blueprint explains all the theoretical details of aesthetic design but does not mention how the scattered parts are floating on their own.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼大剧院的舞台，你曾经奋力战斗过的地方。即使过去了不短的时间，你仍旧能清晰回忆起响彻天际的乐声，以及那场没有尽头的梦。
t_en: The Penacony Grand Theater, a stage you once fought tooth and nail on. It has been a while, but you can still freshly remember the divine melodies and that endless dream.

t_ch: 你发觉自己似乎很难将这简单的草稿与金碧辉煌的大剧院等同起来——这就是艺术天分的差距么，看来还是使用球棒会更适合你。
t_en: You struggle to equate this simple draft with the magnificence of the theater. Is this the difference in artistic genius? It appears that you're still more suited for the ball bat.

t_ch: 非常棒的空间感，使我开始晕3D——评语来自折纸大学。
t_en: Such excellent use of spaces that I got nauseous from it — A comment from Paperfold University.

t_ch: 车站的设计图手稿，系某位筑梦师的作品，你仔细端详拱门的形状，觉得有些眼熟，星穹列车似乎刚好能停泊于此。你开始怀疑这是否属于无名客前辈们的杰作。
t_en: The station's design sketch — the work of a certain Dreamweaver. You carefully examine the archway's form, finding it somewhat familiar, as if the Astral Express would fit like a glove. You start to suspect if this was a masterpiece of a Nameless predecessor.

t_ch: 白日梦酒店，梦境中最复杂的建筑之一——线条太密、画面太满，让人懒得看。
t_en: The Reverie, one of the most complex buildings in the dreamscape — It's got too many lines and too many elements on the page. You can't be bothered to look at it.

t_ch: 多么复杂的设计图，网传存在着一张「只要一直盯着就会让人昏昏欲睡的图片」，或许就是它了。
t_en: What a complicated design. The net has a rumored "drawing that makes you sleepy if you stare at it for too long" image — this could be it.

t_ch: 你仔细端详着这幅手稿，隐约觉得有些眼熟，直到作品下方的小字提醒你这是梦境酒店的设计图，它就和匹诺康尼本身一样令人费解。
t_en: You scrutinize this draft, finding it vaguely familiar, until the fine print at the bottom of the work reminds you that this is the Reverie's design — a drawing as perplexing as Penacony itself.

t_ch: 谐乐学院禁止用储物柜存放乐器，以免造成损伤，但自动乐团似乎不在管控范围内。因此在寂静无人的时候，经常能听到缥缈的乐声从走廊深处传来。
t_en: Charmony Academy prohibits the storage of instruments in lockers to avoid damage, but such rules apparently don't apply to the automated orchestra. This is why one can regularly hear music drift through the corridors in times silent and deserted.

t_ch: 筑梦学院学生专用的储物柜，异常坚固，带有自动清洁功能，据传，这些储物柜是在学院建成初期，由学院教授们亲手制作的。
t_en: A locker exclusively for the Dreamweaver Academy students — exceptionally sturdy and comes with an auto-cleaning function. It's said that these lockers were personally crafted by the school professors when the academy was first established.

t_ch: 听学生说，有些柜门从来没有打开过，配套的钥匙也早已不知所踪。或许柜子里藏着极其危险的生物，也可能是草药和什么关键时刻能救命的物品。
t_en: The students say that some lockers have never been opened, and the keys that go with them have long been lost. Perhaps some dangerous creature is hidden in those lockers, or it could be herbs and items that can deliver salvation in crucial moments.

t_ch: 财富学院学生日常使用的储物柜，与其他学院不同的是，这套柜子设置了超时付费系统。时间观念不强的学生为此缴纳了巨额滞纳金，好消息是，财富学院近期推出了包月套餐。
t_en: A locker typically used by the Fortune Academy students. What makes it different from the other academies is its overtime fee mechanism. Students with a bad sense of time have paid hefty prices for this system. But the good news is, Fortune Academy has recently launched a monthly bundle.

t_ch: 双簧管与长笛的悠扬合奏声在门后响起，你的心情也随之变得如鸟儿般轻快起来。
t_en: The oboe and flute's elegant duet can be heard behind the door. Your mood is lifted like a bird taking to the sky.

t_ch: 你听见了某位教师暴躁的怒吼声，责骂着学生为何跟不上自己的节奏。说真的，你不明白为何这种老师还能在学院里任教。
t_en: You hear the irate barking of some teacher, berating students for not keeping pace. Honestly, you don't know how such teachers are still employed in the academy.

t_ch: 筑梦学院的学生去参加工程实习时，财富学院偶尔会借用他们的大教室——当然是免费借用，财富学院从不干吃亏的事。
t_en: When the Dreamweaver Academy students attend building project internships, Fortune Academy will occasionally borrow their vast classroom — free of charge of course. Fortune Academy never takes a losing deal.

t_ch: 座无虚席的财富学院课室，就连门后都挤满了人。从门缝间传来的声音可以依稀辨认出，似乎是哪位商界名流在此召开经验分享会。
t_en: The Fortune Academy classroom is packed to the brim with students, right up to the door. From the voice seeping through the crack of the door that's ajar, you can make out that some prominent business figure from the industry is sharing their insights.

t_ch: 光线不够明亮的地方会滋生邪恶，在校园题材的故事里，走廊的阴暗角落往往是欺凌频繁发生的场所，好在你并没有见到。
t_en: Evil breeds in places where the light is dim. In stories that happen in school, the dark corners of hallways are typically where bullying occurs. Luckily, you haven't seen anything like that.

t_ch: 虽然不知道这人是谁，但既然能将画像挂在这里，他必定身价不菲吧。
t_en: You don't know who this person is, but if he can hang a painting here, he must be worth a fortune.

t_ch: 眼前这人似乎是折纸大学的某位校董，亦或是匹诺康尼的某位大亨，你不确定——大街上的富人十个有九个都这么穿。
t_en: The person before you appears to be someone on Paperfold University's board, or perhaps a Penaconian magnate, but you're not sure — nine out of ten rich people on the streets dress exactly like he does.

t_ch: 有点眼熟，你总觉得之前好像在哪里看到过这张画像。但既然记不清了，就一定不怎么重要。
t_en: Somewhat familiar... You can't help but feel like you've seen this portrait somewhere. But you can't recall clearly, so it must not be important.

t_ch: 眼前这人似乎是某位在演艺界享有显赫地位的前辈，但你叫不上姓名，她们拍这种照片时穿得都一样。
t_en: This person before you seems to be some veteran who enjoys storied repute in the entertainment world, but you can't recall her name — they all dress the same in photos like this.

t_ch: 格拉克斯教授，隐夜鸫家系前家主，十二梦境的规划人，折纸大学的建立者——也是唯一的正式校长。他是折纸大学的精神领袖，但他身在何处却无人知晓。
t_en: Professor Glaux, former Family head of the Nightingale Family, the planner of the Twelve Dreamscapes, founder of Paperfold University — and the one and only official dean. He is the spiritual leader of Paperfold University, but his whereabouts are unknown.

t_ch: 整齐的礼服，一尘不染的羽毛…所有装束无不彰显着这位校长对细节的追求，而他所创建的折纸大学完美印证了这点。
t_en: The neat formal wear, the impeccable coat of feathers... His entire visage is a testament to this dean's pursuit of detail. His founding of Paperfold University perfectly seals his legacy in this regard.

t_ch: 知更鸟小姐，谐乐学院的荣誉校友，也是一位客座教师——偌大的匹诺康尼，还有谁能不喜欢知更鸟小姐呢？
t_en: Miss Robin, honored alumnus of Charmony Academy, also a guest lecturer — who doesn't love her on Penacony?

t_ch: 据说谐乐学院的学生们会将这张海报挂在床头，每日看三遍。
t_en: It's said that the Charmony Academy students hang this poster over their beds, looking at it three times a day.

t_ch: 梅芙恩女士，鸢尾花家系家主，谐乐学院名誉校长。曾于荒原之中傲然绽放的蓝玫瑰——自她之后，鸢尾开遍原野，美梦姹紫嫣红。
t_en: Lady Maeven, Iris Family Head, Charmony Academy's honorary dean. A blue rose who once bloomed supreme in the wasteland — With her leadership, the Irises spread across the barrens, dreams blooming in kaleidoscopic vibrancy.

t_ch: 你从未见过这位家主本人，但与她相关的故事散落在匹诺康尼的各处，或是一场华丽而不切实际的戏剧，或是一个美好到不真实的梦。
t_en: You've never seen this family head in person, but the stories about her are scattered across Penacony, in a gorgeously surreal theater play, or in an ephemerally splendid dream.

t_ch: 老奥帝，苜蓿草家系家主，财富学院名誉校长。即便眼前的只是一幅画像，你却仿佛已经听见了老奥帝那标志性的笑声——「嚯嗬嗬嗬嗬！」
t_en: Old Oti, Alfalfa Family Head, Fortune Academy's honorary dean. It is only a portrait before you, but you seem to be able to hear his trademark laugh through it — "Ohohoho!"

t_ch: 你在许多地方都见过老奥帝的画像，但财富学院保存的这幅才是原版，价值不菲，而且还是老奥帝亲自卖给折纸大学的。
t_en: You've seen Old Oti's portrait in many places, but this one in Fortune Academy is the original — Nigh priceless, and it was Old Oti who personally sold it to Paperfold University.

t_ch: 艾迪恩先生也有意气风发的岁月——但说实话，比起苏乐达，这张海报或许更适合用来销售牙膏。
t_en: Mr. Aideen was also once a spirited youth — However, compared to selling SoulGlad, the figure in this poster might be more suited to selling toothpaste.

t_ch: 艾迪恩，苏乐达的发扬者，逐梦客们的商业偶像，也是财富学院的客座教授。你的脑海里自动响起了熟悉的歌声——「喝下这瓶苏乐达，快乐美梦速速达！」
t_en: Aideen, SoulGlad's evangelist, the industry idol of dreamchasers, and a guest lecturer at Fortune Academy. A familiar song automatically echoes through your mind — "Grab a bottle of SoulGlad, and make your dreams a blast!"

t_ch: 谐乐学院公认的优秀艺术影片。你与海报中的哈努兄弟对视良久，而后发出了一声很酷的*哼*。
t_en: An excellent art film publicly acknowledged by Charmony Academy. You stare at Brother Hanu in the poster for a long while before you exhale a cool "hmph."

t_ch: 到了某天，学生们即将毕业的时候，他们的肩头会接过各种各样的重担，不知道哈努兄弟坚毅的身影是否仍会留在他们心间。
t_en: One fine day, when the students eventually graduate, their shoulders will bear all sorts of responsibilities. You wonder if Brother Hanu's resolute figure will remain in their hearts.

t_ch: 许多琥珀纪过去了，《钟表小子》系列动画依旧是匹诺康尼不衰的经典。或许它就像折纸大学曾经的辉煌，永远在人们心中。
t_en: Many Amber Eras have passed, but the Clockie cartoon series remains an undying classic on Penacony. Perhaps it's no different from Paperfold University's glory days, forever shining in people's hearts.

t_ch: 谐乐学院的每个人都渴望拍出《钟表小子》那样优秀的作品，却始终没能拿出同等地位的成果…难道是因为克劳克影业的打压？
t_en: Everyone in Charmony Academy dreams of filming such a stellar work like Clockie, but they can never produce something that equals such greatness... Could this be because of harassment from Clock Studios?

t_ch: 《钟表小子》系列动画的经典海报——「滴答！我们的好朋友，愿你能永远笑得开怀！」
t_en: A classic poster from the Clockie cartoon series — "Tick-tock! My good friend, may you always be filled with laughter!"

t_ch: 这一季的《钟表小子》片头曲深入人心——「女士们，先生们，小鸟们~钟表小子和他的朋友们~」
t_en: The opening song for this season of Clockie is truly memorable — "Ladies, gentlemen, birds, Clockie and all his friends!"

t_ch: 「开拓」之旅永远不会结束，你找到自己的下一站了吗，钟表小子？
t_en: The Trailblazing expedition will never end. Have you found your next stop, Clockie?

t_ch: 你的目光偶然扫过这两张毫不起眼的桌子，脑中不知为何缓缓浮现一道白光：前有追逐，所以跳跃很有用——可惜你做不到。
t_en: You cursorily skim across two inconspicuous tables. For some reason, a flash of light gradually appears in your mind: There is pursuit ahead, so jumping is very useful. A pity you can't do that.

t_ch: 他们没有表情，并非活物，仅仅摆出简单的姿势，就成为了一副情感充沛的画面——这便是想象力的作用，也是艺术的根源。
t_en: They are expressionless, inanimate, only capable of posing in simple movements, yet becoming a scene full of emotion — This is the power of imagination, and the source of art.

t_ch: 谐乐学院的人偶。还好教学楼不怎么熄灯，否则得有不少学生会被它们吓醒。
t_en: Charmony Academy's puppets. Good thing the lights in the teaching facility aren't ever fully turned off, or that'd be plenty of students scared awake by them.

t_ch: 结构复杂的仪器，用于揭示普通行星运转的规律。不过在如今这个星际时代，它最多只能算学前玩具。
t_en: A structurally complex device used to reveal ordinary planetary trajectories. But in this current interstellar age, it is a preschool toy at best.

t_ch: 过去，阿斯德纳星系有一伙认为「梦境是个球」的愚昧学者，他们的学说已经被丢进了历史的垃圾堆——现在大家都知道，梦境的形状是乱七八糟的。
t_en: In the past, there were a bunch of ignorant scholars on the Asdana star system who believed that the dreamscape was a ball. Their theories have been discarded into the trash pile of history — everyone knows now that the shape of dreams is a chaotic mess.

t_ch: 你敲敲柜子，没有回应——很好，看来里面并没有什么亟需你拯救的小可怜。
t_en: You knock on the locker, but there is no response. Good, looks like there's nothing inside that requires rescue.

t_ch: 课间存放学生们杂物的储物柜，你在底部翻找，除了年代久远的垃圾就是泛黄的旧书。
t_en: The lockers used by students to store their stuff during breaks. You rummage through the bottom — nothing but outdated trash and yellowed old books.

t_ch: 眼尖的你注意到某个泛黄的书本封面上，写着什么「██王子」的字样，你感到好奇，但课本已被虫蛀得稀碎，你无法得知这位「王子」的真实身份。
t_en: Your sharp eyes notice a faded book cover, written with what seems like "Prince ██." You are curious, but the textbook is so riddled with wormholes that you can't tell the true identity of this "prince."

t_ch: 已经死去的生物，被定格在最美丽的一瞬——如今的梦中存在死亡，却不存在真正的蝴蝶。
t_en: Dead lifeforms are frozen in the moment of ultimate beauty — death is present in the current dream, but real butterflies do not exist.

t_ch: 你很好奇，为什么有些标本会出现在摊位上并被标以高价，有些则只能出现在生物学的课堂上。
t_en: You are curious as to why some samples appear on the shelves and are marked with high prices, but others can only appear in biology classes.

t_ch: 促销中的昂贵礼品，富含时尚感的无用之物。财富学院的学生们整天琢磨着怎么多卖几份出去。
t_en: The expensive gifts on promotion, stylish but ultimately useless. The Fortune Academy students spend their days figuring out how to sell a few more of them.

t_ch: 「恒星能灯泡」「风能风扇」「往人脸上拍的一次性蛋糕」——好像也就最后一个有点用。
t_en: Stellar energy lightbulbs, wind-powered fans, one-time cakes used to shove in people's faces — it appears only the last one is somewhat useful.

t_ch: 和海报不同的是，这些摆饰可以放在任何地方…并且更加昂贵。
t_en: What's different from the poster is that these ornaments can be placed anywhere... and are even more expensive.

t_ch: 摊位售卖抽选盲盒，获得隐藏款的概率是万分之三，前来光顾的客人络绎不绝。
t_en: The stall sells lottery lootboxes, with the probability of getting the hidden version at <unbreak>0.03%</unbreak>. In spite of this, customers still come in droves.

t_ch: 「不要问价格是否虚高，卡片是否保值，重要的是这玩意能刺激经济。」——某位路过的财富学院学生分析道。
t_en: "Don't ask if the price is a little too high, or if the cards can maintain their value. What's important is that this stuff can stimulate the economy." — The analysis of some Fortune Academy student passing by.

t_ch: 影片鉴赏之《鸢尾花房间》——「在梦中体验一部讲述梦中电影的电影吧！」
t_en: Film appreciation: The Iris Room — "Experience a film in the dream that talks about films in the dream!"

t_ch: 在观影梦泡一再加价后，线下观影的人数不断增多，而入场时自行领取的电影海报数量也显著减少。
t_en: After the repeated price hike of the movie dream bubbles, the number of physical movie-goers has continued to increase, and the number of movie posters available for self-pickup upon entry has significantly decreased.

t_ch: 「人生并非只有快乐的事！前往折纸大学的顶点！」你再三确认，这是吹奏部招新的宣传广告，这令你想起某部校园题材的热血青春剧的剧情。
t_en: "Life isn't just only about happy things! Head to the summit of Paperfold University!" You re-confirm that this is a promotional recruitment ad for the Band Club — This reminds you of the plot of a certain school-themed, passionate youth drama.

t_ch: 折纸大学的吹奏部也和电视剧里的一样不务正业吗？你对此感到好奇，却无从考据。
t_en: Is Paperfold University's Band Club just as slack as the one in the drama? You are very curious, but there's no way to verify.

t_ch: 格拉克斯教授，伟大的教育家，筑梦师的先驱，他的功绩使匹诺康尼永世不衰。你问他的功绩是什么？当然是——全部东西，你在梦中能看到的全部东西。
t_en: Professor Glaux, great educator, pioneer of the Dreamweavers. His merits have made Penacony eternal. You ask what his achievements are? They are, of course, everything. All that you can see in the dream is his.

t_ch: 格拉克斯教授的雕像，据筑梦学院传言，考前在这座雕像下放钞票可以不挂科。谐乐学院曝出：该传言是财富学院传出来的，他们的学生每个学期会把大家放的钞票拿走。
t_en: Professor Glaux's statue. According to Dreamweaver Academy's rumors, it will ensure that you pass your exams if you leave money by it. Charmony Academy revealed that this rumor was spread by Fortune Academy — their students would take away everyone's money each semester.

t_ch: 可能会有人好奇校长的藏书品味，但书架上有哪些书可能连校长本人都不知道。
t_en: Someone might be curious about the tastes of the dean's stashed book collection, but even the dean wouldn't know what books there are on the shelves.

t_ch: 不知为何，书架上放着一本《钟表小子与左轮队长》。最有可能的解释是，这是学院从某个开小差的学生手中收缴来的。
t_en: For some reason, there is a Clockie and Captain Revolver book on the bookshelf. The most rational explanation is it was taken from an errant student.

t_ch: 你下意识地去触摸屏幕，但什么也没有发生——这不能怪你，它们长得也太像了！
t_en: You touch the screen without thinking, but nothing happens — You're not at fault for this, as they all look the same!

t_ch: 「笃笃笃——」你敲了敲屏幕，这里会有哈努兄弟钻出来吗？你感到好奇。但回应你的只有电视广告上烦人的罐头笑声。
t_en: "Tap tap tap," you knock on the screen. Will Brother Hanu come out from in there? You're curious. The only response you get is the annoying canned laughter from the TV ad.

t_ch: 印着帕姆笑颜的珍藏款拖鞋，帕姆严令禁止你将它穿出房间。
t_en: These limited-edition slippers, adorned with Pom-Pom's smiling face, come with strict orders from Pom-Pom themselves: You're forbidden from wearing them outside the room.

t_ch: 变频制冷，一秒速冻。商家在一次运营事故中承诺保鲜一个琥珀纪，如今整日祈祷克里珀赶紧落锤。
t_en: With variable-speed cooling and flash freeze capabilities, the manufacturer once promised to preserve items for an entire Amber Era after an operational mishap. Now, they spend their days praying for Qlipoth to bring down the hammer soon.

t_ch: 武装考古学派曾断言人类文明建立在「冰箱的发明」之上，这个论断以一票之差输给了「取火」。
t_en: The Armed Archaeologists once asserted that human civilization was founded upon the "invention of the refrigerator," but this claim lost by a single vote to the "discovery of fire."

t_ch: 底部一侧的使用说明上写着：禁止长时间打开、禁止倒置使用、禁止关入大象（分成多少步都不行）。
t_en: The instructions on the bottom side read: Do not leave open for extended periods, do not use upside down, and do not attempt to lock an elephant inside (no matter how many pieces you slice it into).

t_ch: 人类初次踏上太空时，菜谱里只有单调的压缩食品，如今太空旅行中的饮食已经远远超出他们的想象。
t_en: When humans first set foot in space, their menus consisted only of bland, compressed foods. Now, the cuisine available during space travel has far surpassed anything they could have imagined.

t_ch: 这款饮料机附带的打气功能为任何液体注入二氧化碳，你甚至可以用它自己制作「苏打豆汁儿」。
t_en: This beverage machine comes with a carbonation feature that injects carbon dioxide into any liquid. You can even use it to make your own "mung bean soda."

t_ch: 你总是在吃完一包零食前就忍不住拆封下一包，于是桌子变成了这副样子。
t_en: You can't help but open another snack before finishing the first one, and now the table looks like this.

t_ch: 这盆花的花期只有一天，但原产地的「一天」约等于开拓历的「十年」。你至今没有见到过它们凋谢的样子。
t_en: This flower blooms for just one day, but in its homeland, a "day" lasts about ten years by our Trailblaze Calendar. You've never seen it wither.

t_ch: 衣柜里整齐排列着款式相同的套装。
t_en: The wardrobe is neatly arranged with identical outfits.

t_ch: 「今天该如何穿搭呢？」你显然没有这样的烦恼。
t_en: "What should I wear today?" Clearly, you don't have that kind of dilemma.

t_ch: 上面的房间，以后再来探索吧。
t_en: How about we explore the rooms ahead of us later?

t_ch: 「闭嘴」精心编排的特调饮品单，上面写满了冷笑话。
t_en: The special drink menu, meticulously arranged by Shush, is packed with dry jokes.

t_ch: 很想复述给你听，但旁白要保持客观超然的严肃性，并不是写冷笑话的地方。
t_en: I wish I could recount them, but the narrator must remain objective and serious. This isn't the right time for jokes.

t_ch: 「旁白」字数太多，就会变得「白白旁旁」——与「白白胖胖」谐音，令人忍俊不禁。
t_en: If the narration gets too long, it turns chubby — which then turns flappy and funny.

t_ch: 前面的车厢，以后再来探索吧。
t_en: How about we explore the cars ahead of us later?

t_ch: 只要还在开拓，车厢就会无限延伸……
t_en: As long as the Trailblazing continues, the express cars will extend endlessly...

t_ch: 无人打扰的僻静角落，适合独处、阅读和沉思。
t_en: In a secluded corner undisturbed by anyone, it's a perfect place for solitude, reading, and contemplation.

t_ch: 镜子里浮现出一张引人歆羡的脸庞。你简单调整了角度和表情，现在它看起来更加完美了。
t_en: A captivating face appears in the mirror. You adjust your angle and expression slightly, and now it looks even more perfect.

t_ch: 很遗憾，这面镜子没有实装捏脸功能。
t_en: Unfortunately, this mirror doesn't come with a face-sculpting feature.

t_ch: 你和镜子里的自己猜拳，最后打成了平手。
t_en: You and your reflection play rock-paper-scissors, and in the end, it's a tie.

t_ch: 推理社、机动球社、话剧社…望着塞满视野的茫茫多摊位，你忽地心生冲动——既然所有人都有资格提出申请，为什么学校里不能有属于你的社团？
t_en: The Deduction Club, the Roboball Club, the Drama Club... looking out at the sea of stalls as far as the eye can see, an urge wells up within you — If anyone can submit a request, why can't there be a club for you in this school?

t_ch: 没错，所以先来做个规划吧，要是可以的话，你想在折纸大学组建什么社团？
t_en: That's right, so it's time to draw up a plan first. If you could, what club would you form at Paperfold University?

t_ch: …文学社。
t_en: ...A literature club.

t_ch: 你就是如此地热爱着文字，并且坚持认为每所学校都必须拥有自己的文学社。书籍是我们的精神食粮，在梦里当作真正的食物也没什么问题。
t_en: You love literature very much, and maintain your firm belief that every school should have their own literature club. Books are food for our soul, and they can even be used as actual food in the dream.

t_ch: 继续想象吧，你想要组建一个什么样的文学社呢？
t_en: Keep going. What sort of literature club would you like to form?

t_ch: 让人心跳不已的！
t_en: One that sends the heart aflutter!

t_ch: …好吧，你选择了相当危险的方向，并注定要为这个想法付出代价，包括但不仅限于：完成布置的诗歌作业、定期与部员交流观点，以及目睹不愿察知的真相。
t_en: ...All right. You've chosen a rather dangerous direction, and are determined to pay the price for this decision, including but not limited to: completing poetry assignments, conducting regular discourse with club members, and witnessing hard truths.

t_ch: 社团建立得很顺利，但最终，出于各种各样的原因，整个社团只剩下你与另外一名部员。尽管如此，她仍旧对你不离不弃，从未想过离开。
t_en: The club is formed without trouble, but eventually, due to various reasons, you and one other person are all that's left of the entire club. Even so, she insists on staying with you, never leaving your side.

t_ch: 你组建的文学社将一直、一直持续下去，直到永远。
t_en: The literature club you formed will always, always carry on, unto eternity.

t_ch: ——可喜可贺，可喜可贺。
t_en: ...Sincere congratulations.

t_ch: 安静看书的就好。
t_en: Just read a book quietly.

t_ch: 对，这才是文学社该有的活动，你喜欢普通的生活，不想被卷进那些稀奇古怪的麻烦事中。于是你设定了极其严格的规则，谨慎挑选着部员。
t_en: Yes, this should be the sort of activity befitting a literature club. You like the ordinary life, not being whisked into those bizarre, life-altering troubles. And so you write up particularly stringent rules and meticulously select club members.

t_ch: 只有一名学生通过了考核，她的任何方面都符合你的要求——普通身高，普通的短发，戴着普通的眼镜，丢在人群里很快就会消失不见的那种。
t_en: Only one student makes the cut, everything about her meeting your requirements — ordinary height, ordinarily short hair, and wearing a pair of ordinary glasses. The sort that would instantly disappear in a crowd if you thrust her in there.

t_ch: 你期待的社团生活就这样普通地开始了，没有任何玫瑰色，有的只是漫无止境的阅读时光。你们坐在教室里安静看着书，偶尔说几句话，仅此而已。
t_en: The club life you hoped for starts just like that. No fanfare, no roses, just endless reading time. You two sit in the classroom, reading quietly, occasionally exchanging words, but that's it.

t_ch: 直到某天，有个高喊着「要让世界变得更热闹」的学生闯了进来，强行占据了这间教室，并将你们吸纳为了她的新社团成员。
t_en: Until one day, some student barges in, shouting "Saving The World By Overloading It With Fun," then takes over this classroom and subsequently absorbs you two as her new club members.

t_ch: 你组建的文学社至此总算完成了它的使命，成为了新世界最初的那块垫脚石。
t_en: The literature club you formed formally concludes its duty at this moment, becoming the first stepping stone for a new world.

t_ch: ——可喜可贺，可喜可贺。
t_en: ...Sincere congratulations.

t_ch: …音乐社。
t_en: ...Music club.

t_ch: 作为永远行走在潮流前沿的开拓者，你当然不会错过时下最流行的乐队题材。音乐就是有着能让所有人沉浸其中的魅力，你相信那些美妙旋律正是你内心的呼喊。
t_en: As a Trailblazer who is always at the forefront of style, of course you won't miss out on the trendiest band-themed bandwagon. Music has that immersive allure, and you believe that those melodious rhythms are an outcry from your soul.

t_ch: 继续想象吧，你想要组建一个什么样的音乐社呢？
t_en: Keep going. What sort of music club would you like to form?

t_ch: …喜欢轻松的音乐。
t_en: ...I like chill music.

t_ch: 没错，参加社团活动是为了放松，而不是非得要在大赛上获得金奖，你很清楚自己并非是以音乐为终身志向的人，享受生活才是你所需要的。
t_en: That's right. Club activities gotta be chill, not to join some major competition for some shiny award. You are certain you aren't someone whose lifelong ambition is in music — enjoying life is what you really need.

t_ch: 幸运的是，你找到了四名志同道合的同学，共同组成了轻音乐队。你成功过上了吃着茶点悠闲度日的生活，不需要为卖票或成员关系而有任何烦恼。
t_en: Luckily, you find four like-minded students and form a light music band. You successfully experience an idyllic life of chill music and crumpets, not having to worry about selling tickets or drama between members.

t_ch: 这才是乐队的初衷，不是吗？不用时刻担心会有人来结束这美好的一切。
t_en: This is what a band should be, no? No need to be mired in constant worry that someone is here to end this beautiful lifestyle.

t_ch: 再后来，你们毕业了，乐队也就自然而然地解散了。你很清楚学历始终与演奏水平成反比，选择正常毕业，也就意味着放弃了乐队梦想。
t_en: Later on, you all graduate, and the band naturally disbands. You are aware that your grades and your music skills are the antithesis of each other. Choosing to graduate normally marks an end to your band dream.

t_ch: 无论如何，这是你短暂的校园生活中最为美好的回忆，值得反复回味。
t_en: No matter what, this was the most treasured memory of your school stint — a scene worth reminiscing on.

t_ch: ——可喜可贺，可喜可贺。
t_en: ...Sincere congratulations.

t_ch: …偏爱古典吹奏乐。
t_en: ...I prefer classical woodwind and brass.

t_ch: 此前你从未意识到，自己原来是个如此怀旧的人，但每当看到那些深沉的古典乐器时，你的心中都会油然涌起澎湃的向往之情。
t_en: You never thought of yourself as a nostalgic person before this, but every time you see those profound classical instruments, your heart wells up with a surge of longing.

t_ch: 于是，你决定向梦想发起冲击，哪怕会遭遇无数次失败。你颤巍巍地在申请书上写下「吹奏乐部」几个字，递交给学生会，然后发现……
t_en: So, you decide to pursue your dream, even if you will be met with countless setbacks. You write down "Concert Band Club" on the request form with trembling hands, submit it to the student council, and later realize...

t_ch: ——折纸大学竟然已经存在「吹奏乐部」了！！！
t_en: ...Paperfold University already has a Concert Band club!!!

t_ch: 好吧，看来有人和你想到了相同的事。无论如何，你后续都可以去会会他们。
t_en: All right, looks like someone else thought of the same thing as you. No matter what, you can seek them out in the future.

t_ch: …「{NICKNAME}社」。
t_en: ...The {NICKNAME} Club.

t_ch: 不错。格拉克斯校长有自己的雕像，老奥帝的自传都不知道重印了多少版，作为匹诺康尼的大股东、伟大的盛会巨星，折纸大学里存在以你为主题的社团有什么问题吗？
t_en: That's right. Dean Glaux has his own statue, and Old Oti's biography has been reprinted who-knows how many times. As Penacony's major shareholder and awesome Festive Superstar, Paperfold University shouldn't have a problem with a club themed after yourself, right?

t_ch: 没错，所以继续想象吧，你想要组建一个什么样的「{NICKNAME}社」呢？
t_en: That's right. So, keep going. What sort of {NICKNAME} club do you want to form?

t_ch: 学术性的。
t_en: Academic.

t_ch: 学生的义务是学习，难得有这样的机会，不如就让部员们研究你的冒险经历与开拓旅程好了。你召集了一批对无名客感兴趣的学生，组成了关于你的学术社团。
t_en: A student's duty is to study. With such a rare opportunity, why not have the members delve into your adventurous experiences and trailblazing expeditions? You gather a group of students interested in the Nameless and form an academic club about you.

t_ch: 你们定期开展交流会，产出会议论文，组织学术讲座…渐渐地，你开始习惯在学校里的生活，这里安宁而美好，不需要面对任何未知的风险。
t_en: You regularly hold discussions, write discourse theses, organize academic seminars... Slowly, you start to get accustomed to school life — it is peaceful and beautiful here, no need to face any unknown threats.

t_ch: 开拓者成为了「开拓学科」的研究者，也就变得不再「开拓」了。但至少，你收获了稳稳的幸福。
t_en: If a Trailblazer becomes a researcher for Trailblazing, they won't be Trailblazing anymore. At the very least, you have acquired safe and stable happiness.

t_ch: ——可喜可贺，可喜可贺。
t_en: ...Sincere congratulations.

t_ch: 娱乐向的。
t_en: Entertainment oriented.

t_ch: 你很清楚，没有人喜欢看严肃的学术报告，最高效的宣传方式永远是走下沉市场，于是你号召你的部员，仿照睡蕉小猴的模式拍摄了数十个以你为主角的短视频。
t_en: You are clear that no one likes stuffy academic reports. The most effective promotional methods are always aimed at the lower end of the market funnel. So, you call upon your members, and, "inspired" by the Slumbernana Monkey's style, film dozens of short videos where you're the main character.

t_ch: 出乎意料的是，这些视频在折纸大学的论坛上爆火，你的影像成为了著名的网络迷因，作为表情包在学生们的手机里疯传。好，你现在是传说了。
t_en: What is surprising is that these videos go viral on Paperfold University's forum. Your image becomes a popular internet meme, and the emojis made out of it spread like wildfire among students' phones. Good, you are a legend now.

t_ch: 传奇永不灭。在这个由梦境与潜意识组成的混沌世界里，你获得了永生。
t_en: Legends can never die. In this chaotic world formed from the dreamscape and subconscious, you have attained immortality.

t_ch: ——可喜可贺，可喜可贺。
t_en: ...Sincere congratulations.

t_ch: 还是算了吧。
t_en: Never mind.

t_ch: 组建社团可太累了，不符合你的节能原则，还是当个不出头的旁听生会更适合你。
t_en: Forming a club is too exhausting, and doesn't align with your energy conservation principles — It's better to be a guest student who doesn't rock the boat.

t_ch: 好了，幻想时间结束了。为你鼓掌一次。想得很好，但下次请别站在学校门口发呆了。
t_en: All right. Daydream time is over. I'll applaud you once — great imagination, but don't do it at the school gate next time.

t_ch: 你好，请问是对音乐感兴趣吗？我是折纸大学吹奏部的部长，欢迎来社团参观！
t_en: Hello, interested in music? I'm the president of Paperfold University's Concert Band Club. You're welcome to take a look!

t_ch: 不用紧张啦，就算从来没接触过乐器也没关系，我们会安排专门的教学课程。这里是轻松享受音乐的地方，不需要为任何多余的事烦恼。
t_en: Don't worry. It's okay even if you've never touched an instrument. We will arrange for professional lessons. This is a place where you can enjoy music without stress. You don't have to be concerned about anything else.

t_ch: 拿不到金奖也没关系吗？
t_en: So it's okay even if you don't win first prize?

t_ch: 哈哈，不知道你是从哪听来的传闻。虽然…以前是有段时间，大家很努力地训练，想要拿到好的名次…但都是过去的事啦。
t_en: Haha, I don't know where you heard this. Though... there was such a time when everyone worked hard to train and obtain good results... but that was in the past.

t_ch: 毕竟大部分成员参加社团，都是出于好玩的心理，强行以专业的水准来要求所有人，既不现实，也没什么意义，对不对？
t_en: Most members join clubs out of sheer fun. It's not very practical to force professional standards on everyone, and it's pretty meaningless too, no?

t_ch: 不会被社团前辈指责吗？
t_en: I won't be reprimanded by the club's seniors?

t_ch: 当然不会！成员间的关系非常重要，要是有人欺负你，请随时跟我说！
t_en: Of course not! Member relations are very important. If someone bullies you, you must let me know!

t_ch: 毕竟大部分成员参加社团，都是出于好玩的心理。以能力水平的高低来划分地位，未免太不近人情了，我不喜欢这样。
t_en: Most members join clubs out of sheer fun. If we established our roles based on proficiency, it'd be too draconian. I don't like that.

t_ch: 吹奏部的原则是「兴趣至上」，很符合折纸大学的校训吧。
t_en: The mantra of the Concert Band Club is "interest above all," very in line with Paperfold University's vision.

t_ch: 你是最称职的部长！
t_en: You're the best president ever!

t_ch: …噢！不太明白为什么突然说这个，既然是夸奖，就先谢谢你啦。
t_en: ...Oh! I don't know why you suddenly said that, but I appreciate the compliment.

t_ch: 有兴趣的话，可以来我们的社团参观！
t_en: If you're interested, you can take a look at our club!

t_ch: 快看啊！悬在舞台上面的…我从来没见过这么大的气球，还是发金光的！
t_en: Come take a look! Hanging above the stage... I've never seen such a huge balloon before, and it's shiny too!

t_ch: 那个形状…是只鸟？我这个角度看不太清，拍好了给我也瞅瞅！
t_en: That shape... is it a bird? I can't tell from this angle, take a picture and send it to me, tweeet!

t_ch: 顺着说话者的视线望过去，你见到的是……
t_en: Following the speaker's line of sight, you see...

t_ch: <size=28>飘浮在中央舞台上方的折纸小鸟气球！</size>
t_en: <size=28>An origami bird balloon floating above the center of the stage!</size>

t_ch: 不得不承认，你也没见过这么大的气球，并且它既不是「折纸」做成的，也绝对算不上是「小鸟」。你对如何称呼它产生了疑惑。
t_en: You have to admit, you've never seen such a giant balloon before. It's not folded with paper, and it definitely isn't as small as an Origami Bird. You struggle with what to call it.

t_ch: 好吧，名字并不重要，你知道它是个巨大的气球，和折纸小鸟有着相似的外观，这样就够了。比起纠结这个，还是看看不远处的舞台吧。
t_en: All right. The name isn't important. You know it's a giant balloon, and it looks like an Origami Bird. That's good enough. Rather than struggling over what it's called, how about looking at the stage?

t_ch: 伫立在校园中央的折纸小鸟气球，以骄傲的姿态向所有到访者展示着它嘹亮的歌喉。
t_en: Situated in the center of the campus, the Origami Bird balloon demonstrates its sonorous song for all to see in all its proud glory.

t_ch: 请容我向你介绍——这位就是折纸小鸟中的传奇巨星，引领当代音乐潮流的「鸟王」！
t_en: Allow me to introduce to you — the legendary superstar among Origami Birds, the pioneer of trending music: The Bird King!

t_ch: 不要问为何如此重量级的角色没有在《钟表小子》系列动画中出现过，不断推出新角色才是长篇故事能保持生命力的诀窍。
t_en: Don't ask why such a crucial character didn't appear in the Clockie cartoon series — the introduction of new characters is the secret formula to staying relevant in long-formula storytelling.

t_ch: 啾~啾啾~啾啾啾啾~这位客人，我们在跟随舞台的旋律练习合唱啾，会吵到你吗？
t_en: Tweet! Tweet tweet~ tweet tweet tweet tweet~! Hello! We are practicing our chorus with the stage's melody. Is it disturbing you?

t_ch: 小鸟，你可以唱歌。
t_en: No, you can keep singing.

t_ch: 啾啾！那我们就开始正式合唱了！
t_en: Tweet tweet! Then let us begin the chorus in earnest!

t_ch: <size=36>吼——我不要——吃——香蕉——猴——吼吼——</size>
t_en: <size=36>Roaaarrrr, we doooon't want to eaaat bananaaaa monkeyyyys roaarrrrr</size>

t_ch: 呼…喊出来后舒服多了啾。
t_en: Phew... I feel much better after yelling it out, tweet.

t_ch: 你怎么还会核嗓的？
t_en: How'd you know how to do a death growl?

t_ch: 啾！这可是艺术家的自我修养，要成为我的粉丝，就趁现在啾！
t_en: Tweet! It's the self-cultivation of an artist. If you want to be my fan, now's the time, tweet!

t_ch: 你这也太拼命了吧…
t_en: You're really going all in for it...

t_ch: 啾！不懂得欣赏艺术的家伙，我要拉黑你啾！
t_en: Tweet! A plebe who can't appreciate art. I'm gonna ban you, tweet!

t_ch: 啾~啾啾~啾啾啾啾~这位客人，我们在跟随舞台的旋律练习合唱啾，会吵到你吗？
t_en: Tweet! Tweet tweet~ tweet tweet tweet tweet~! Hello! We are practicing our chorus with the stage's melody. Is it disturbing you?

t_ch: 小鸟，你可以唱歌。
t_en: No, you can keep singing.

t_ch: 啾啾！那我们就开始正式合唱了！
t_en: Tweet tweet! Then let us begin the chorus in earnest!

t_ch: 啾~啾~啾~啾~客人哟~跟着我的节奏，扭个腰，转个头，提提肩，抬抬手~动作要温柔，力道要足够~不要偷懒啾！
t_en: Tweet tweet~ tweet tweet tweet tweet hello! Follow my beat, twist your waist, turn your head, shrug your shoulders, and raise your head! Your movements must be gentle! Your strength must be ample! No goofing off, tweet!

t_ch: 我是提醒运动小助手~记得每天坚持锻炼啾！
t_en: I'm your exercise reminder assistant! Remember to keep up with your daily training, tweet!

t_ch: ——给我出去！谐乐学院是培育艺术天才的地方，容不得你这种愚钝的学生！
t_en: Get out! Charmony Academy is a place for nurturing artistic talents and has no space for dim-witted students like you!

t_ch: 你忽然听见了震耳欲聋的咆哮声，随后一个皮皮西学生被赶到了走廊上。
t_en: You suddenly hear an ear-splitting bellow, which is followed by a Pepeshi student being thrown out into the corridor.

t_ch: …不就是合唱时候走了几次音嘛，至于发这么大脾气。
t_en: All I did was sing a few notes out of tune during the chorus... Did he really have to fly into such a terrible rage?

t_ch: 嘿，同学，我看你在这也挺突兀的，莫非你也是被老师赶出来罚站的？
t_en: Hey, fellow student, I see you're standing awkwardly around, too. Were you also chased out by your teacher and made to stand here as punishment?

t_ch: 是啊，都搁这站好久了。
t_en: Yea, I've been standing here for quite a while.

t_ch: 太好了，原来是站友啊！这下不用担心在走廊里丢人了，来，咱们聊聊天。
t_en: Perfect, we can be buddies! It's not so embarrassing if we have company. Come, let's chat to pass the time.

t_ch: 撒一个谎需要用无数个谎言来圆，于是你被迫和这位同学聊起了你为数不多的校园经历——当然是在来来往往的众多意味深长的目光下。
t_en: Well, it seems that one lie begets more lies, which is why you're forced to talk about your limited campus experience with this student under the stares of numerous students as they pass by.

t_ch: 天呐，那不是盛会巨星嘛？到底是犯了什么错，居然被老师赶出来罚站。
t_en: My goodness, isn't that the Festive Superstar? What did {F#she}{M#he} do to be sent out to stand in the corridor?

t_ch: 嘘…不该问的事情别问！这样的大人物能被罚站，肯定是出大事了！
t_en: Shhh... You shouldn't ask about these sorts of things! If a big shot like {F#her}{M#him} is being punished, it means that {F#she}{M#he} must have done something really bad!

t_ch: 你看，那两个人罚站的样子好滑稽，好像睡蕉小猴哦。
t_en: Look, the two of them look so comical standing there, like a couple of Slumbernana Monkeys.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，太好笑了，我得联系新闻社来报道这件事，先拍个照片……
t_en: Hahaha, that's funny! I must contact the News Society to report about this, but not before I take a picture first...

t_ch: 就这样，在过往人群的指指点点与窃窃私语声中，你度过了这节漫长而又煎熬的课堂。你不明白为何最开始自己要陪这位可怜的同学罚站，但至少——
t_en: And so, amid the constant pointing and murmuring of passers-by, you manage to endure the endless torment that is this class. You still don't understand why you had to be punished alongside this poor student in the first place, but at least...

t_ch: 你的校园体验变得更完整了！
t_en: Your campus experience is all the more fulfilling because of it!

t_ch: 我是来监督你的！
t_en: I'm here to supervise you!

t_ch: …说什么呢！最近学校在抓教学质量，我是来做检查的。这位同学，你被老师拉出来罚站，是不是犯了什么错误啊？
t_en: What are you talking about? Since the school has been paying more attention to teaching quality as of late, I am here to conduct an inspection. Why have you been sent out of class? What did you do?

t_ch: 噫！没、没什么大事…就是合唱时候站错了地方，唱跑调了而已！真的！
t_en: Me? N—Nothing much... I just stood in the wrong position during choir practice and sang a few notes out of tune. Honest!

t_ch: 我本来是男高音部的成员，按理说应该站后排。可我个子矮，在后面就看不见指挥了，所以我就混进了低音部，就跟着周围人唱错了……
t_en: As a tenor, I am supposed to stand in the back row of the choir. However, I'm so short that I wouldn't be able to see the conductor if I stood at the back. While I tried to blend in with the baritones after that, I got distracted and sang the wrong notes...

t_ch: 明白了，你继续站会吧。
t_en: I see. Well, carry on with your punishment, then.

t_ch: 是！我会按老师要求，站到这节课结束！
t_en: Got it! I will follow my teacher's instructions and stand here until this lesson is over!

t_ch: 接受你的检查后，这位认真的同学站得更加笔直了。好吧，为了学校的教学质量，必须要有人做出牺牲。你在心底默默地向他道歉，然后——
t_en: Upon concluding your inspection, you realize that this earnest student is standing even straighter than before. Ah well, for the sake of the school's teaching standards, someone must be made an example of. You silently apologize to him in your heart, and then...

t_ch: 你没做错，回教室去吧。
t_en: You've done nothing wrong. Go back to class.

t_ch: 是！那我就…回座位了？
t_en: Alright! Then I'll be... returning to my seat?

t_ch: 尽管有些迟疑，这位认真的同学仍旧按你的要求，结束了这堂课的罚站。你很难不去想象后续究竟会发生什么样的故事，但现在最好还是——
t_en: Despite some hesitation, the conscientious student concludes the punishment according to your instructions. While you can't help but wonder what the consequences of such an action will be, for now, it's best that you...

t_ch: 趁没人发现，赶快开溜吧。
t_en: Sneak away while the coast is clear.

t_ch: 一台设计十分荒诞的电话机被固定在这里，等待路过的好奇宾客拿起它的听筒。
t_en: A peculiarly designed telephone is fixed in place, waiting for curious passersby to pick up its receiver.

t_ch: 叮铃铃——叮铃铃！
t_en: *ring*... *ring*!

t_ch: 晚上好，尊贵的客人！您今天是否有兴趣打一通神奇的「窥梦电话」？
t_en: Good evening, esteemed guest! Would you be interested in making a Dreampeek Call today?

t_ch: 「窥梦电话」是什么？
t_en: What is a "Dreampeek Call"?

t_ch: 问得好，问得好！初来乍到的客人，还不知道「窥梦电话」的神奇之处！
t_en: A wonderful question! Please allow me to introduce you to the wonders of the Dreampeek Call, dear new guest!

t_ch: 只要您投入一点点{RUBY_B#信用点}苜蓿币{RUBY_E#}，窥梦电话就能自动检索你的通讯录，找到您正在熟睡的某位朋友——
t_en: Just insert a small amount of {RUBY_B#credits}Alfalfa credits{RUBY_E#}, and the phone will automatically search your contacts for a sleeping friend and allow you to listen to their dreams...

t_ch: ——然后让您听到对方正在梦到什么！怎么样，是不是很有趣？是不是很心动？
t_en: ...and let you know what your friend is dreaming about! Isn't it thrilling and fun?

t_ch: 听着不太…道德？
t_en: It doesn't sound very... ethical, right?

t_ch: 窥梦电话突然爆发出一阵脆生的「叮铃」声——你听不出那是嘲弄还是爽朗的笑。
t_en: The phone suddenly emits a crisp ring, leaving you uncertain whether it is a taunt or a hearty laugh.

t_ch: 叮铃铃，「讲道德的匹诺康尼派对客」——我得承认，您的笑话确实很有品！
t_en: *ring* An "ethical partygoer in Penacony"! I must admit that your joke is quite refined!

t_ch: 这下听懂了。
t_en: Now I understand.

t_ch: 拨通「窥梦电话」。
t_en: Dial the Dreampeek Call.

t_ch: 嘟…嘟…嘟……
t_en: *dial tone*

t_ch: 叮铃…叮铃…叮铃……
t_en: *ring*... *ring*... *ring*...

t_ch: 窥梦电话已拨通——您将会听到：藿藿的梦境。
t_en: The Dreampeek Call connected. You are about to hear Huohuo's dream.

t_ch: 藿藿！听说绥园里又冒出来好多鬼火！我们再去探险吧。
t_en: Huohuo! I heard that heliobi are haunting Fyxestroll Garden again! We should go check it out.

t_ch: 走。正好我随身带着葫芦，这次非得把那些爱吓人的家伙一网打尽不可！
t_en: Let's go. I've got my gourd with me anyway, and I'll make sure to bust all these prankster jerks this time!

t_ch: 不愧是藿藿——十王司最可靠的判官，这次就全靠你了……
t_en: Oh Huohuo, you really are the most reliable judge of the Ten-Lords Commission. I'm counting on you...

t_ch: 判官藿藿。随我去趟幽囚狱，身负「不赦十恶」的凶犯正等候受审。
t_en: Judge Huohuo. Please accompany me to the Shackling Prison. The criminal accused of the Ten Unpardonable Sins is about to be sentenced.

t_ch: 明白。寒鸦大人，狱中环境阴暗，路途岐长，请跟在我后方，以防遭遇凶险。
t_en: Understood. Madam Hanya, it's dark in the jail, and the walk is long. Please follow behind me, in case we run into trouble.

t_ch: 嗯，此案还真是棘手，多亏司中有你在，否则还不知要拖到何时……
t_en: Mm, this case is quite tricky. Good thing you were at the commission, or who knows how long this would have taken...
...Huo... huo...

t_ch: 可笑，区区岁阳，也敢扰我清静。十王司判官在此，邪祟之物还不速速退散！
t_en: Pathetic, a puny heliobus dares to disrupt my peace and quiet. A Ten-Lords Commission judge stands before you! All evil spirits should turn tail and start fleeing!

t_ch: 嘿，你这小怂包，有种把刚才的话再对着老子说一遍？
t_en: Hah, you sure are a bratty one. How about saying that one more time to my face?

t_ch: ——呜哇啊啊！
t_en: ...Uwaaahh!

t_ch: 通话到此结束。你不确定藿藿究竟是从梦中惊醒，还是被尾巴大爷给强硬拉了起来——毕竟这种梦话，要是不小心说出口可就糟糕了。
t_en: The call ends here. You're not sure if Huohuo was rudely awoken from a dream, or she was dragged away by Mr. Tail to parts unknown — it'd be disastrous if such somniloquy was accidentally spoken.

t_ch: 叮铃铃——感谢您使用窥梦电话服务！欢迎您下次再来！
t_en: *ring* Thank you for using the Dreampeek Call service! Please come back next time!

t_ch: 一台设计十分荒诞的电话机被固定在这里，等待路过的好奇宾客拿起它的听筒。
t_en: A peculiarly designed telephone is fixed in place, waiting for curious passersby to pick up its receiver.

t_ch: 叮铃铃——叮铃铃！
t_en: *ring*... *ring*!

t_ch: 晚上好，尊贵的客人！您今天是否有兴趣打一通神奇的「窥梦电话」？
t_en: Good evening, esteemed guest! Would you be interested in making a Dreampeek Call today?

t_ch: 「窥梦电话」是什么？
t_en: What is a "Dreampeek Call"?

t_ch: 问得好，问得好！初来乍到的客人，还不知道「窥梦电话」的神奇之处！
t_en: A wonderful question! Please allow me to introduce you to the wonders of the Dreampeek Call, dear new guest!

t_ch: 只要您投入一点点{RUBY_B#信用点}苜蓿币{RUBY_E#}，窥梦电话就能自动检索你的通讯录，找到您正在熟睡的某位朋友——
t_en: Just insert a small amount of {RUBY_B#credits}Alfalfa credits{RUBY_E#}, and the phone will automatically search your contacts for a sleeping friend and allow you to listen to their dreams...

t_ch: ——然后让您听到对方正在梦到什么！怎么样，是不是很有趣？是不是很心动？
t_en: ...and let you know what your friend is dreaming about! Isn't it thrilling and fun?

t_ch: 听着不太…道德？
t_en: It doesn't sound very... ethical, right?

t_ch: 窥梦电话突然爆发出一阵脆生的「叮铃」声——你听不出那是嘲弄还是爽朗的笑。
t_en: The phone suddenly emits a crisp ring, leaving you uncertain whether it is a taunt or a hearty laugh.

t_ch: 叮铃铃，「讲道德的匹诺康尼派对客」——我得承认，您的笑话确实很有品！
t_en: *ring* An "ethical partygoer in Penacony"! I must admit that your joke is quite refined!

t_ch: 这下听懂了。
t_en: Now I understand.

t_ch: 拨通「窥梦电话」。
t_en: Dial the Dreampeek Call.

t_ch: 嘟…嘟…嘟……
t_en: *dial tone*

t_ch: 叮铃…叮铃…叮铃……
t_en: *ring*... *ring*... *ring*...

t_ch: 窥梦电话已拨通——您将会听到：拉帝奥教授的梦境。
t_en: The Dreampeek Call connected. You are about to hear Professor Ratio's dream.

t_ch: 清晰的水花声从另一侧传来，你猜这位追求纯粹思维的教授此刻正泡在浴缸里。
t_en: The distinct sound of splashing water can be heard from the other side. You're guessing that professor who pursues pure thought is being submerged in the bathtub right now.

t_ch: 《论忆质原理》…隐夜鸫家系居然把这种基础的理论书籍作为见面礼，可笑。
t_en: On Memoria Principles ...To think that the Nightingale Family would choose a book with such rudimentary theory as a gift. How laughable.

t_ch: 《虫皇陨落之谜》…博识学会什么时候也开始写这种校园科普读物了。
t_en: Mystery of the Imperator Insectorum's Fall ...When did the Intelligentsia Guild start writing academic material for high schools?

t_ch: 《原始博士的隐秘身份》…公司真以为我会对街边小报里的故事感兴趣？
t_en: Dr. Primitive's Secret Identity ...Does the IPC really think I'd be interested in such street tabloids?

t_ch: 无趣，就没有什么难题能刺激下我的大脑吗？
t_en: How banal. Is there no conundrum to stimulate my brain?

t_ch: 教授，入浴剂的效用时间就要到了，请问您接下来想用什么种类的？
t_en: Professor, the bath soak is almost past its effective period. Might I ask what type you wish to use next?

t_ch: …不错，你提了一个值得思考的好问题。
t_en: ...Not bad. You brought up a question worthy of thinking about.

t_ch: …看来即便是最富有智慧的头脑，也会在诸如「今天想吃什么」这样的问题上产生疑惑。
t_en: ...It appears that even the most intelligent mind also contemplates on questions like "What to eat today."

t_ch: 叮铃铃——感谢您使用窥梦电话服务！欢迎您下次再来！
t_en: *ring* Thank you for using the Dreampeek Call service! Please come back next time!

t_en: *dial tone*

t_ch: 叮铃…叮铃…叮铃……
t_en: *ring*... *ring*... *ring*...

t_ch: 窥梦电话已拨通——您将会听到：知更鸟的梦境。
t_en: The Dreampeek Call connected. You are about to hear Robin's dream.

t_ch: 老师，轰炸的声音越来越近了，我们…会没事吗？
t_en: Miss Teacher, the explosions are getting closer. We... will be okay, right?

t_ch: 别害怕，那些噪音是有人按错了琴键。让我们来演奏正确的旋律，引导他们吧。
t_en: Don't worry. Those sounds are just someone pressing the wrong keyboard keys. Let us play the correct melody to guide them.

t_ch: 跟着我的节奏哦。三、二、一……
t_en: Follow my rhythm now, six, seven, eight...

t_ch: 美妙的歌声盖过了全部杂音，世界仿佛在此刻彻底安静了下来。
t_en: A splendid voice blankets all cacophonies — it is as if the world has gone completely silent in the moment.

t_ch: 我们的愿望…正在一点一点实现。对吧？
t_en: Our wishes... are coming true little by little. Isn't that right?

t_ch: 电话里的声响消失了，取而代之的是漫长的寂静。
t_en: The sound on the phone vanishes, only to be replaced by a pregnant pause of silence.

t_ch: 你难以想象知更鸟在梦醒时的心情，只觉得有股空洞而酸楚的滋味在胸口渐次扩散开来。
t_en: You can't begin to imagine Robin's feelings when she awakens from the dream. You feel only a hollow, heart-wrenching ache spreading through your chest.

t_ch: 叮铃铃——感谢您使用窥梦电话服务！欢迎您下次再来！
t_en: *ring* Thank you for using the Dreampeek Call service! Please come back next time!

t_ch: 叮铃…叮铃…叮铃……
t_en: *ring*... *ring*... *ring*...

t_ch: 窥梦电话已拨通——您将会听到：波提欧的梦境。
t_en: The Dreampeek Call connected. You are about to hear Boothill's dream.

t_ch: 热乎乎的炖菜做好了，快来尝尝。尼克？又在给波提欧讲那些牛仔故事啦？
t_en: The steamed veg is ready. Try it while it's hot. Nick? You telling Boothill those cowboy tales again?

t_ch: 我不懂，为什么非得离开家乡，就为了找那个抢了他们东西的帮派报仇？
t_en: I don't get it. Why leave ya hometown just to find that gang who robbed them and take revenge?

t_ch: 这里有吃的，有喝的，还有家人和朋友在。丢掉这一切，去追一个可能根本不存在的人，值得吗？
t_en: There's food 'n' drink, and family 'n' friends here. To throw all that away and pursue someone who might not even exist, is it worth it?

t_ch: 哈哈，你现在当然不明白。复仇不用找借口，只有恶徒才需要理由。
t_en: Haha, of course you don't get it. There ain't no reason needed for vengeance. Only baddies need reasons.

t_ch: 但浪费美味的晚餐同样要受惩罚。快点，收拾好桌子。波提欧，别忘了你的牛奶！
t_en: But wasting a delicious dinner is just as easily punishable. Come now, tidy up the table. And Boothill, don't forget your milk!

t_ch: 嘿！我早过了喝牛奶的年纪，没劲的玩意还是留给老家伙吧。
t_en: Hey! I'm long past the age to be drinking milk. Leave that boring milk to the old and stuffy.

t_ch: 你这混小子，不喝牛奶怎么长得高，我给你倒杯子里了啊。
t_en: You rascal, you won't grow taller if you don't drink milk. I'll pour some into your cup.

t_ch: 别！那是我刚拿的麦芽果汁！
t_en: No! That's the malt juice I just got!

t_ch: 通话在炖菜的沸腾声中悄然结束。你从未想象过波提欧能有如此欢闹的情景——这件事就像他会喝牛奶那样不可思议。
t_en: The call fizzles amidst the stewing sounds of boiled vegetables. You never knew of a time when Boothill would be so happy — a time just as unfathomable as him drinking milk.

t_ch: 叮铃铃——感谢您使用窥梦电话服务！欢迎您下次再来！
t_en: *ring* Thank you for using the Dreampeek Call service! Please come back next time!

t_ch: 嘟…嘟…嘟……
t_en: *dial tone*

t_ch: 叮铃…叮铃…叮铃……
t_en: *ring*... *ring*... *ring*...

t_ch: …非常抱歉，目前没有对象能够接通您的「窥梦来电」。请确保您的通讯录中有至少一位朋友正处在深度睡眠中，然后再试一次。
t_en: ...I'm sorry, but there is currently no one available to connect you to your Dreampeek Call. Please ensure that at least one of your friends in your contacts is in deep sleep and try again.

t_ch: 房间灯光的开关，在拉动时会发出「咔哒」一声。
t_en: The switch for the room lights emits a "click" when flicked.

t_ch: 「咔哒」，柔和的光线充盈了整个房间。
t_en: "Click," and soft light fills the entire room.

t_ch: 「咔哒」，你说要有黑夜——于是就有了黑夜。
t_en: "Click," you say there should be night — so night comes.

t_ch: 「咔哒」，你说要有光——于是就有了光。
t_en: "Click," you say there should be light — so light appears.

t_ch: 「咔哒」，天黑请闭眼。
t_en: "Click," close your eyes as it gets dark.

t_ch: 「咔哒」，天亮请睁眼。
t_en: "Click," open your eyes as it gets bright.

t_ch: 「咔哒」，「咔哒」，「咔哒」。你反复按动开关，灯泡却置若不闻。
t_en: "Click," "Click," "Click." You repeatedly press the switch, but the lightbulb remains unresponsive.

t_ch: 随后，一股焦糊味充满了你的鼻腔。恭喜！你的好奇心击溃了灯泡的承载能力，报修单将是你对它最后的判词。
t_en: Then, a burnt smell fills your nostrils. Congratulations! Your curiosity has overwhelmed the lightbulb's capacity, and the repair ticket will be your final verdict on this mechanism.

t_ch: 「闭嘴」闻讯赶来，一番折腾后总算修好了电灯——你都不知道它还有这个手艺。
t_en: Shush rushes in upon hearing the news, and after some fuss, it finally fixes the light — who knew it had such skills?

t_ch: 从它电子屏荧光的频闪里，你竟看出一丝真挚的惊讶。
t_en: From the flickering glow of its electronic screen, you can detect a hint of genuine surprise.

t_ch: 亲爱的无名客，现在是一个科技昌明的时代。列车常用的电灯，理论使用寿命接近一个琥珀纪。
t_en: Dear Nameless, we are in an age of technological advancement. The lightbulbs commonly used on the Express have a theoretical lifespan approaching an entire Amber Era.

t_ch: 可它在您的手下，没撑过四回合就需要维修了…也许是因为您「开拓」的意志，也使得这盏无生命的电灯，走上了「开拓极限」的道路吧。
t_en: Yet, in your hands, it barely lasts four rounds before needing repairs... Perhaps your Trailblazing spirit has nudged this lifeless light onto a path of "Extreme Trailblazing."

t_ch: 这个故事告诉我们：要量力而行，不要盲目攀「灯」没有把握的山峰。
t_en: This story reminds us to act within our limits and not to recklessly chase the "light" without thought to your actions.

t_ch: 希望它以后能够吸取教训。
t_en: Hopefully, it will learn from this experience.

t_ch: 淋浴间正在呼唤你——花洒、壁挂架和每一块瓷砖都注视着你，邀请你开始一场酣畅淋漓的淋浴。
t_en: The bathroom calls to you — the faucet, wall-mounted rack, and every tile watch you, inviting you to indulge in a hearty and refreshing shower.

t_ch: 那就冲个澡吧。
t_en: Then, let's take a shower.

t_ch: 水温逐渐稳定，热气开始升腾……
t_en: The water temperature gradually stabilizes, and steam begins to rise...

t_ch: 水珠蹦跳，前所未有的自由感充盈在你的心间。此刻，你仿佛一只在山涧清泉中徜徉的小猴，纯洁无瑕的心里没有一丝忧虑。
t_en: Water droplets bounce, and an unprecedented sense of freedom fills your heart. At this moment, you feel like a little monkey frolicking in a clear mountain spring, your pure heart unburdened by any worries.

t_ch: 心灵得到净化，人生得到重启，你感觉自己焕然一新了！
t_en: Your soul is cleansed, your life replenished, and you feel utterly refreshed!

t_ch: 暂时不想洗澡。
t_en: You don't want to take a shower just yet.

t_ch: 一个小悖论：越脏的人越需要洗澡，越干净的人越不需要洗澡。因此越常洗澡的人越脏，越不常洗澡的人越干净。
t_en: Here's a little paradox: the dirtier a person is, the more they need to shower, while the cleaner a person is, the less they need to shower. Therefore, the ones who shower most often are the dirtiest, and those who shower less frequently are the cleanest.

t_ch: 水温逐渐稳定，热气开始升腾……
t_en: The water temperature gradually stabilizes, and steam begins to rise...

t_ch: 水珠蹦跳，清扬的流水声与你相和。你不由得引吭高歌，歌声在浴室湿润的水汽中回响，有如天籁般悦耳。
t_en: Water droplets bounce, and the clear sound of flowing water harmonizes with you. You can't help but burst into song, your voice echoing in the bathroom's humid atmosphere, as melodious as heavenly music.

t_ch: 浴室歌王出浴了！你又唱了一嗓子，差点给自己听笑了。
t_en: The bathroom's star singer has appeared! You sing another line, almost laughing at yourself.

t_ch: 水温逐渐稳定，热气开始升腾……
t_en: The water temperature gradually stabilizes, and steam begins to rise...

t_ch: 水珠蹦跳，你用脊背迎接着流水的冲击，心却如磐石巍然不动。此刻，你有如瀑布之下领悟绝学的武学宗师，用区区肉身对抗大自然的伟力。
t_en: Water droplets bounce off you as you embrace the flow with your back, your heart steady as a rock. In this moment, you feel like a martial arts master, enlightened beneath the waterfall, using your body to withstand the mighty forces of nature.

t_ch: 喝——开拓之力，突破！你出关了，银河间将再度响彻球棒侠的传说！
t_en: Drink — Harness the trailblaze power and ascend! You've emerged, and the legend of Galactic Baseballer will once again echo throughout the cosmos!

t_ch: 水温逐渐稳定，热气开始升腾……
t_en: The water temperature gradually stabilizes, and steam begins to rise...

t_ch: 水珠蹦跳，悬浮的肥皂泡「啵」地破碎，散出一朵水花姗姗落下。你乐此不疲地吹向指间的肥皂膜，一串五彩斑斓的泡泡鱼贯而出。
t_en: Water droplets bounce, and a floating soap bubble pops with a "plop," sending a splash of water falling gently. You joyfully blow towards the soap film between your fingers, and a string of colorful bubbles flows out in succession.

t_ch: 你玩得太久，手指都起了褶皱。
t_en: You've played for so long that your fingers have wrinkled.

t_ch: 电脑机箱组全天候待机，等待着你的来临。识别到你虹膜的注视，它在你接近前便早早解锁开机。
t_en: The computer case is always on standby, waiting around for your arrival. It unlocks and powers on as soon as it detects the gaze of your irises.

t_ch: （查看邮箱）
t_en: (Check your mailbox)

t_ch: 几封广告，几封账单，几封不知所云的垃圾邮件……
t_en: A few advertisements, a few bills, and some incomprehensible spam...

t_ch: 没有重大新闻，也没有亟待解决的紧急事件，每颗恒星都运转在自己的轨道上，今天也是宇宙里日复一日的平淡一天。
t_en: No major news, and no urgent matters to address. Every star is orbiting in its own path, making today just another mundane day in the universe.

t_ch: （启动游戏）
t_en: (Start Game)

t_ch: 由于星际和平公司的版权限制，此处无法向你展示实机游玩画面。你自己脑补一下好了。
t_en: Due to copyright restrictions from Interastral Peace Corporation, we cannot show you actual gameplay footage here. You'll have to imagine it yourself.

t_ch: 《公司<unbreak>1800</unbreak>》
t_en: IPC 1800

t_ch: 这是一款模拟经营类游戏，你将扮演「血锦之纪」的某位普通职员，在那个伟大却残酷的时代，书写属于自己的传奇！
t_en: This is a simulation managerial game where you will play as an ordinary employee in the Gloryblood Era, writing your own legend in that great yet cruel time!

t_ch: 坊间传言，公司会招募这款游戏的高阶玩家，这传言害得不少人将青春消耗在了这款游戏上。
t_en: Rumor has it that IPC will recruit high-level players from this game, a rumor that has caused many to waste their youth on it.

t_ch: 《狐中之狼•极》
t_en: Wolf Among Foxes: Extreme

t_ch: 这是一款角色扮演类游戏，你将扮演一位「三劫时代」的曜青袍哥，刑满释放后，混迹在日新月异的曜青街头，活络筋骨、整顿帮派，并逐步揭晓昔日大哥遇害的真相。
t_en: This is a role-playing game where you play as a Robe Brethren from the Three Sufferings Era. After serving your sentence, you navigate the ever-changing streets of the Yaoqing, stretching your limbs, reorganizing your gang, and gradually uncovering the truth behind your former leader's murder.

t_ch: 游戏标题隐含着欠妥当的表述，因此制作方收到了联盟的抗议。制作方进行了深刻反省，现在新发售的版本已经改名为《只狐》了——意为「形单影只的狐人」。
t_en: The game's title contained an inappropriate implication, leading to protests from the Alliance. The developers reflected deeply, and the newly released version has been renamed Sole Fox — meaning "a solitary foxian."

t_ch: 《完蛋，我被军团包围了！》
t_en: Oh No, I'm Surrounded by the Legion!

t_ch: 这是一款多人合作第一人称射击游戏，以高速射击和热血战斗为主要卖点。你将扮演被围困的巡海游侠，在热血偾张的战局中和队友不懈合作，突出重围！
t_en: This is a multiplayer cooperative first-person shooter, featuring high-speed shooting and intense battles as its main attractions. You will play as a beleaguered Galaxy Ranger, collaborating tirelessly with your teammates to break through the siege in this exhilarating conflict!

t_ch: 可能是现在市面上最流行的多人线上游戏。但星穹列车的网络信号始终不太稳定，再加上运营方的服务器质量一言难尽…你还是比较喜欢玩单人战役模式。
t_en: It's probably the most popular multiplayer online game on the market right now. However, the network signal on the Astral Express has always been unstable, and the quality of the game's servers leaves much to be desired... you still prefer playing the single-player campaign mode.

t_ch: 没什么想玩的…
t_en: Not much you want to play...

t_ch: 你兴致勃勃地扫视了一圈游戏列表，突然感到索然无味。
t_en: You excitedly scan through the catalog list, only to suddenly feel bored.

t_ch: 你都已经花钱买了游戏，为什么还要花时间去玩呢？
t_en: You've already paid for the game, so why spend time playing it?

t_ch: （看看「超智能病毒」）
t_en: (Look at the Supersmart Virus)

t_ch: 「超智能病毒」吃掉了你回收箱里的所有垃圾文件。
t_en: The Supersmart Virus has consumed all the junk files in your recycle bin.

t_ch: 不用谢，我的好伙伴～
t_en: No need to thank me, my good partner~

t_ch: （关机）
t_en: (Shut down)

t_ch: 该休息了。
t_en: It's time to rest.

t_ch: 你右键查看「超智能病毒」的属性，发现它占用的存储空间大大增加了。
t_en: You right-click to check the properties of the Supersmart Virus and find that its storage space has significantly increased.

t_ch: 看来需要为它制定一个健康饮食计划了。
t_en: Looks like you need to create a healthy diet plan for it.

t_ch: 「超智能病毒」看完了你存储的所有影片，催促你再下载一些。
t_en: The Supersmart Virus has finished watching all the videos you stored and urges you to download more.

t_ch: 你建议它自己联网搜索，并警告它不许偷翻你的相册。
t_en: You suggest it search for itself online and warn it not to snoop through your photo album.

t_ch: 「超智能病毒」正在加固系统防火墙……
t_en: The Supersmart Virus is reinforcing the system's firewall...

t_ch: 「超智能病毒」凭一己之力阻止了系统不合时宜的自动更新。
t_en: The Supersmart Virus single-handedly prevented the system from unnecessary automatic updates.

t_ch: 「超智能病毒」在《超级开拓小子》中的段位又有所提升，希望它没开挂。
t_en: The Supersmart Virus has leveled up in Super Trailblaze Kid, and you hope it hasn't been cheating.

t_ch: 说起来，你到底算不算「超智能病毒」的家人？一想到开挂的狗东西总会被问候家人，你就觉得有些不安…希望它没开挂！
t_en: So, do you consider yourself family to the Supersmart Virus? Just thinking about how cheaters always get a bad rap makes you a bit uneasy... Let's hope it's not cheating!

t_ch: 陪伴你出生入死的球棒，银河球棒侠最好的伙伴。
t_en: The baseball bat that accompanies you through thick and thin, the best partner of the Galactic Baseballer.

t_ch: 这是一根身经百战的球棒，你几乎用它打过棒球外的一切。
t_en: This is a battle-hardened bat, and you've used it to hit everything but baseballs.

t_ch: 它曾刺穿过你的胸膛，因此成了你最贴心的好友。
t_en: It once pierced your chest, which is why it became your bosom friend.

t_ch: 偶尔会有些烫手，除此之外挑不出缺点。
t_en: It occasionally gets a bit hot to handle, but other than that, it has no flaws.

t_ch: 这顶礼帽曾横渡星海，跨过漫长岁月。如今它沉眠在列车里，安详得如同归乡的游子。
t_en: This top hat has traversed the starry seas and crossed countless years. Now it lies dormant on the Express, as peaceful as a traveler returning home.

t_ch: …你决定让它休息一会儿。
t_en: ...You decide to let it rest for a while.

t_ch: 有时你会忽然想和它说说话，但你知道不会有回应。
t_en: Sometimes you suddenly want to talk to it, but you know it won't respond.

t_ch: 身为一支笔，似乎更应该呆在书桌上。
t_en: As a pen, it seems more fitting to stay on the desk.

t_ch: 空荡荡的收藏架，静静等待着藏品的入驻。
t_en: The empty display shelf quietly awaits the arrival of collectibles.

t_ch: 造物引擎等身手办回暖纪念首发款，1/<unbreak>1000</unbreak>比例。
t_en: First edition of the life-size Engine of Creation figure celebrating the return of warmth, available at 1/<unbreak>1000</unbreak> scale.

t_ch: 这里只有一个头颅。原因很简单——全身太大了，放不下。
t_en: There's only a head here. The reason is easy — its full body is too large to fit.

t_ch: 你想起了在造物引擎上狂奔的曼妙时光，它的拳头真的很带劲。
t_en: You remember the wonderful times you spent running on the Engine of Creation. Its fists were really powerful.

t_ch: 上至天道行运，下至人势沉浮，穷目而观无穷。
t_en: From the workings of heaven to the rise and fall of humanity, observing the infinite.

t_ch: 据说穷观阵能推衍出时间长流中发生的一切。
t_en: It is said that the Matrix of Prescience can predict everything that occurs in the flow of time.

t_ch: 你询问它今日运势，它却沉默不语。大概因为这只是一个摆件吧。
t_en: You ask it about fortune for today, but it remains mute. Perhaps because it's just a decorative item.

t_ch: 它并非简单的雕塑，而是一艘能够正常行驶的航模。
t_en: It is not a simple sculpture, but a model ship that can operate along a route as per its designations.

t_ch: 天舶司会定期举办微型星槎竞速赛，活动口号是：「快快快！冲冲冲！星槎战士在行动！」
t_en: The Sky-Faring Commission regularly holds micro-starskiff racing competitions, with the slogan: "Quick quick quick! Rush rush rush! Starskiff Guardians take action!"

t_ch: 这架微型星槎的载重量不足一个帕姆，还是不要指望用它做逃生舱了。
t_en: This micro starskiff has a payload capacity of less than one Pom-Pom, so it's best not to expect it to serve as an escape pod.

t_ch: 魔方秘社为「潜在高价值用户」特别赠送的纪念品，旨在鼓励用户随时随地枘凿六合。
t_en: The Hex Club presents this special souvenir to "potential high-value users" to encourage them to explore the Hexanexus anytime, anywhere.

t_ch: 只要人类还躺着睡觉，便携式掌上游戏机就还有市场——便携版枘凿六合的设计者如此坚信着。
t_en: As long as humanity continues to sleep lying down, there will be a market for portable handheld gaming consoles — so believes the designer of the portable Hexanexus.

t_ch: 没有一丝使用痕迹，已经有点吃灰了。
t_en: It shows no signs of use and is already gathering dust.

t_ch: 钟表小子站在收藏架上，露出温暖亲切的笑容。
t_en: Clockie stands on the display shelf, wearing a warm and friendly smile.

t_ch: 具备闹铃功能，能够用一百句钟表小子经典台词叫你起床。
t_en: Equipped with an alarm function, it can wake you up with a hundred classic lines from Clockie.

t_ch: 「滴~答~{NICKNAME}！早上好！美梦小镇新的一天开始了~」
t_en: "Tick~ tock~ {NICKNAME}! Good morning! A new day has begun in Dreamville~"

t_ch: {TEXTJOIN#87}交接完成后附赠的实体纪念品，相比梦中，它带给你更加真实的触感。
t_en: The physical souvenir provided after the {TEXTJOIN#87} was handed over to you offers a more tangible sensation than that in your dreams.

t_ch: 你仔细打量这艘属于自己的船，船身的每道弧度在你眼里都是如此完美。
t_en: You carefully examine your personal ship. Every curve of the hull appears perfect in your eyes.

t_ch: 你轻轻拨动船头，模型在悬浮力场里滴溜溜地打了个转。
t_en: Gently nudging the bow, the model spins gracefully in the hovering force field.

t_ch: 你想起了在垃圾桶王国的冒险奇旅，想起了并肩作战的伙伴们。
t_en: You recall the adventurous journey in the Trashcan Kingdom and think of your comrades who fought alongside you.

t_ch: 它们赴汤蹈火，深信你能够带领自己推翻塔塔洛夫，赢回被剥夺已久的尊严与自由。
t_en: They ventured through hell and high water, firmly believing you could lead them to overthrow Tatalov and regain their long-lost dignity and freedom.

t_ch: 虽然只是一场梦，醒来还是很感动。
t_en: Although it was just a dream, waking up still leaves you moved.

t_ch: 垃圾糕正在你的房间里休息。看到你来了，它向你友好地道了一声「喵」。
t_en: The trash cake is resting in your room. Upon seeing you, it greets you with a friendly "Meow."

t_ch: 摸摸它。
t_en: Pet it.

t_ch: 金属桶盖手感冰凉又光滑…它不会是什么冷血动物吧？
t_en: The metal lid feels cold and smooth... It can't be a cold-blooded creature, can it?

t_ch: 喵~
t_en: Meow~

t_ch: 不摸它。
t_en: Don't pet it.

t_ch: 你想到了「回家故意不摸宠物」挑战，并决定实验一番——垃圾糕怡然自得地舔着尾巴，完全不顾你火热的眼神。
t_en: You recall the "Challenge of Ignoring Your Pet on Purpose" and decide to give it a try — while the trash cake happily licks its tail, completely unfazed by your eager gaze.

t_ch: 喵~
t_en: Meow~

t_ch: 你输了，在这场精神角力里。
t_en: You lost this mental tug-of-war.

t_ch: 垃圾猫糕的桶里是什么呢？
t_en: What's inside the trash cat cake's can?

t_ch: 你敲了敲桶身，发出清脆的声响…你最终还是克制住了掀开桶盖的冲动。
t_en: You tap the can, producing a crisp sound... but ultimately, you suppress the impulse to lift the lid.

t_ch: 喵~
t_en: Meow~

t_ch: 哼哼！哼哼哼！
t_en: Oink! Oink oink!

t_ch: 你开始装修房间后，这只扑满也随即拓展了自己的探索范围。如今它在你的房间里漫步，好奇地嗅着每一个角落。
t_en: After you started redecorating your room, this trotter also expanded its exploratory range. Now it wanders around your room, curiously sniffing every corner.

t_ch: 跟它玩一会儿吧。
t_en: Why not play with it a little?

t_ch: 哼~哼哼哼！
t_en: Oink~ Oink oink oink!

t_ch: 它热情地蹭向你的手背，对你的关注感到十分兴奋。
t_en: It eagerly rubs against your hand, feeling very excited by your attention.

t_ch: （拍拍它的脑袋）
t_en: (Pat it on the head)

t_ch: 哼，哼哼~
t_en: Oink, oink oink~

t_ch: 扑满发出兴奋的声音，时不时拱起鼻子做出回应。它很开心。
t_en: The trotter lets out a squeal of excitement, scrunching up its nose frequently in response. It's very happy.

t_ch: （捏捏它的鼻子）
t_en: (Squeeze its nose)
Oink... oink oink...

t_ch: 扑满的鼻子格外敏感，有什么东西正在酝酿......
t_en: The trotter's nose is extremely sensitive. Something seems to be brewing...

t_ch: 哼唧！
t_en: OINK!

t_ch: 一个巨大的喷嚏...幸亏以太灵没有鼻涕。
t_en: It is a gigantic sneeze... Thankfully Aether Spirits don't have snot.

t_ch: （揪揪它的耳朵）
t_en: (Tug on its ear)

t_ch: 哼，哼哼~
t_en: Oink, oink oink~

t_ch: 扑满的逃跑天赋同样反映在它身体的各个部位上。你试图抓住它灵巧的耳朵，手速却怎么都不够用。
t_en: The trotter's instinct for escape is reflected in every part of its body. You try to catch its dexterous ears, but your hand is just not fast enough.

t_ch: 哼，哼哼~
t_en: Oink, oink oink~

t_ch: 听见了吗？那是胜利的哼声。
t_en: Did you hear that? That's the oink of victory.

t_ch: （摸摸它的屁股）
t_en: (Pat its butt)

t_ch: 哼，哼哼！哼——
t_en: Oink, oink oink! Oink...

t_ch: 银河中有句箴言：「扑满的屁股摸不得」，即便它又小又可爱。
t_en: There is a saying across the cosmos: "One must never touch a trotter's butt," no matter how cute or small it is.
